name: MiniMax Coding Plan (minimax.io)

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: MiniMax's published privacy materials state they generally do not transfer personal data outside Singapore, but they may transfer data to affiliates and/or third parties outside Singapore when needed. No EU-only processing or EU-region routing option was found in primary-source documentation for the API platform.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: The privacy policy says personal data will cease to be retained as soon as it is reasonable that it is no longer required. The API docs also state the text-to-speech interface is stateless and that the model does not store user data; some generated/cloned voice artifacts are described as temporary and must be used within 168 hours (7 days) to be kept permanently.
dataUsage.details: I found no primary-source API policy expressly stating whether API customer data is or is not used for model training. The publicly available app/web terms grant MiniMax a broad license over user contributions and generated content, but no API-specific training statement or customer opt-out was found.

notes: MiniMax publishes privacy and cookie policies and identifies Nanonoble Pte. Ltd. in Singapore as operator, with an Article 27 EU representative, but I found no public DPA/AVV, SCC commitment, sub-processor list, EU-only residency option, or verified security certifications in primary-source materials. API/platform docs confirm pay-as-you-go pricing and include some product-level storage behavior, but they do not clearly state whether API customer data is used for training.
